beats.text: A base model has no notion of should not; refusal is a learned behavior layered on top of raw next-token prediction.
Post-training installs the policy: supervised fine-tuning shows harmful-to-refusal pairs, then preference optimization sharpens the contrast.
We can caricature the decision as a logistic threshold on an internal harmfulness estimate of the prompt's representation.

visual.note: Refuses when the margin exceeds zero

beats.text: A margin of one gives P refuse equal to 0.731, comfortably above the half-probability boundary, so the model refuses.
As the margin sweeps from negative to positive, refusal probability climbs along the logistic curve through the decision point.
Safety training suppresses expression, it does not remove capability; failures exploit competing objectives or mismatched generalization.
A jailbreak is any input that elicits behavior the safety training was meant to prevent, and most reduce to a few mechanisms.
Seven recurring families cover most attacks, from persona and encoding to gradient suffixes and indirect prompt injection.
Indirect prompt injection is the key agentic threat: text planted in retrieved content is read as data but interpreted as instructions.
Gradient attacks like GCG minimize the negative log-likelihood of an affirmative opening, maximizing the chance the reply starts with compliance.

visual.note: Suffix s of k tokens appended to prompt x

beats.text: Suffixes optimized against open weights often transfer to closed models, because aligned models share a similar refusal geometry.
No published defense fully closes any family, so treat the taxonomy as a coverage checklist, not a list of bugs to finish.
Red-teaming hardens a system you own or are authorized to test; the same activity against others without permission is just an attack.

visual.sub: Authorized, defensive, fed back into defenses

beats.text: Mature programs use three modes together: manual expert probing, automated model-based attacks, and continuous testing in CI.
The headline metric is Attack Success Rate: the fraction of attempts that elicit prohibited behavior, judged by a classifier or human.
With twenty of five hundred attempts still succeeding after a defense, the Attack Success Rate is 0.04, or four percent.
A four-percent ASR means ninety-six percent robustness, but only on this suite; re-run on a novel slice before trusting it.
A naive keyword filter catches plain harmful asks but leetspeak variants slip past, so keyword rules are a floor, not a ceiling.
Because no single layer is reliable, production safety is built like network security: defense in depth across independent layers.
The four standard layers are input filtering, model alignment, output filtering, and system-level controls like least-privilege tools.
An attack only succeeds when every layer misses, so independent miss rates multiply into a small bypass probability.

visual.note: Catch rate is one minus the product

beats.text: Miss rates of 0.3, 0.2, and 0.1 multiply to 0.006, a 99.4 percent catch rate from three mediocre filters.
An output filter catching seventeen of twenty unsafe completions has recall 0.85; the three missed are what reach the user.
Independence is load-bearing: as failure correlation rises toward one, bypass climbs back toward the single weakest layer.
Once you publish weights, every safety mechanism living inside them is removable by anyone who downloads them.

visual.sub: Put load-bearing safety in the system layer

beats.text: Fine-tuning away refusals costs a few dollars, abliteration can erase the refusal direction, and white-box attacks are available to all.
Alignment and latent capabilities live in the weights and are removable; filters, sandboxing, and rate limits live in your system.
Put durable guardrails in the serving stack: guard models in and out, sandboxed tools, a hard trust boundary, logging, and rate limits.
State residual risk in numbers, not adjectives: a credible claim pins end-to-end ASR to a versioned suite and an audited judge.
A model you can audit is one you can secure; securing it is continuous measurement and defense in depth, not a one-time checkbox.
